title: Gemini 代码助手新功能：代理模式、IDE 改进和 Gemini CLI 更新
author: Blanca Delgado Parra
content:
Agent Mode widely available, IDE improvements, and Gemini CLI updates


Transform the way you build with powerful agentic features from Gemini Code Assist’s agent mode, now available to everyone both in VS Code and IntelliJ, with a continuously improved experience across your IDE and CLI.


Agent Mode within your IDE

Gemini Code Assist's Agent Mode helps you tackle complex, multi-step tasks with an intelligent, collaborative approach.

For example, imagine you want to refactor the model, view and controller of a shopping cart to display applied discount codes. This would typically require changing multiple files. With Agent Mode, you simply describe the goal, and Gemini will propose a detailed plan for you to review and approve before any code is changed, giving you complete control over every step.

By combining the power of AI with your expertise, Agent Mode helps you achieve a more efficient workflow and produce better code, while saving you significant time and effort.


VS Code

Agent Mode was previously launched in the Insiders channel for VS Code. During this time, our team has been working hard to improve the experience and we’re happy to share that Agent Mode is now in Preview and available to everybody.

Agent Mode in VS Code is a reimagined chat experience in Code Assist powered by the Gemini CLI to make developers more productive. It expands the capabilities of simple command/response interactions inside Code Assist chat by providing support for multiple file edits, full project context, built-in tools and integration with ecosystem tools (using MCP) with integrated Human in the Loop (HiTL).

With this launch, several enhancements to Gemini Code Assist Agent Mode in VS Code have been made:

  • Edit code changes directly using the integrated Gemini Inline diff, allowing you to visually highlight code modifications directly in your editor.

  • Improved clarity for code edits through inline diffs in chat.

  • Persistent agent mode in chat history for a consistent experience.

  • Streamlined and intuitive user-friendly quota updates.

  • Real-time shell command output.

  • Support for batched tool call approvals and edits.

  • Faster UI performance and rendering.

  • Agent mode toggle state is preserved between IDE restarts (i.e. if you turn the toggle to on, it stays on until turned off).

IntelliJ

Agent Mode is now also available on Stable release in IntelliJ.

You can take advantage of this by using the Gemini Code Assist chat in Agent Mode to complete complex, multi-step tasks and goals with complete control over every change. Before making any modifications, the agent will present a plan for your review. You can edit, ask for changes, approve, or deny any suggested changes. This collaborative approach combines the power of AI with your expertise, resulting in better code and a more efficient workflow.

You can also choose to enable auto approve mode to let the agent act on your behalf. Once the agent is done you can review and roll back changes as you see fit.

IDE improvements

Boost your productivity by a refined Gemini Code Assist experience on your IDE.

You can now have more control over your interactions with chat due to the new ability to stop in progress chat responses, so you can immediately halt undesired long or errant chat responses. And with the newly introduced thinking tokens for IntelliJ, you can get more insights into Gemini's thought process before it delivers a response.

Additionally, write code faster and more efficiently with our latest improvements to code completion for VS Code, boosting the performance of code completion suggestions.

Last but not least, many of the previously launched preview features are now generally available.

  • Revert to checkpoints

  • Add selected code snippets to context

  • Prompt Gemini Code Assist with selected terminal output

  • Specify filenames in your workspace

  • Exclude files from Gemini Code Assist use


This will ensure stability and reliability for your daily workflows.


Gemini CLI updates

Since the launch of Gemini CLI’s launch late June, the community has contributed over 2800 pull requests, submitted about 3400 issues and received more than 70.000 stars. The Gemini CLI has been rapidly evolving to continue to bring the power of Gemini directly into your command line. In the last month, there were some important launches and updates focused on security, privacy, and continued refinement.

Gemini CLI GitHub Actions has now launched. It is a no-cost, powerful AI coding teammate for your repository. It acts both as an autonomous agent for critical routine coding tasks, and an on-demand collaborator you can quickly delegate work to. It’s now in beta, widely available.

Triggered by events like new issues or pull requests, it works asynchronously in the background, using the full context of your project to automatically handle tasks. It knows your code, understands what you want to do, and gets it done.

Three key use cases covered today are:

  • Intelligent issue triage

  • Accelerated pull request reviews

  • On-demand collaboration

The Gemini CLI has also been updated with a deeper integration into VS Code. It becomes context-aware of the files you have open and can access selected text. This allows the CLI to provide targeted and contextually relevant suggestions, as the tool understands precisely what you're working on at the moment. With native in-editor diffing capabilities, you can see suggested changes side-by-side and edit them before accepting. To start using this, use the Gemini CLI version 0.1.20 or higher and a one-time setup command. The update aims to improve developer workflows by providing more relevant and flexible suggestions directly within the IDE.

In addition to Gemini CLI Github Actions and VSCode deeper integration, we have launched many improvements and fixes with the goal of improving Gemini CLI’s reliability and consistency, along with other launches such as Custom Slash Commands, allowing you to define reusable prompts for streamlining interactions with Gemini CLI.

You are a vital part of shaping the future of the Gemini CLI, which is why we want to be transparent about our plans and share our progress with you every step of the way. For that, we have published a public roadmap for Gemini CLI v1.

We hope these changes make for a better coding experience and make you more productive.

Try Gemini Code Assist for free, and learn more about our recent updates in our release notes.
aiSummary: Google 推出了 Gemini 代码助手的重大更新，包括 VS Code 和 IntelliJ 中全面推出的代理模式，以及增强的 IDE 功能和扩展的 Gemini 命令行工具能力。
oneSentenceSummary: Google 推出了 Gemini 代码助手的重大更新，包括 VS Code 和 IntelliJ 中全面推出的代理模式，以及增强的 IDE 功能和扩展的 Gemini 命令行工具能力。
summary: 本文详细介绍了 Google Gemini 代码助手的最新进展，重点关注三个关键领域：代理模式、IDE 改进和 Gemini 命令行工具更新。代理模式现已在 VS Code 和 IntelliJ 中全面推出，使开发人员能够与 AI 协作处理复杂的多步骤编码任务，并提出详细的计划以供用户审查和批准。此功能旨在提高工作流程效率和代码质量。文章重点介绍了代理模式的几个 VS Code 特定增强功能，例如集成的内联差异和持久的聊天记录。此外，VS Code 和 IntelliJ 的 IDE 改进包括停止进行中的聊天响应、IntelliJ 的“思考令牌”以及更快的代码完成。最后，涵盖了开源 Gemini 命令行工具的重大更新，特别是 Gemini 命令行工具 GitHub Actions 的发布，用于执行诸如问题分类和拉取请求（PR）审查之类的自主编码任务，以及更深入的 VS Code 集成，以实现上下文感知的建议和原生编辑器内差异比较。这些更新共同旨在提高开发人员的生产力并简化编码工作流程。

mainPoints.point: Gemini 代码助手的代理模式现已全面推出，实现了 AI 驱动的多步骤任务自动化。
mainPoints.explanation: 代理模式允许开发人员描述复杂的编码目标，Gemini 提出详细的计划以供审查和批准，从而显著提高效率和对多文件更改的控制。

mainPoints.point: IDE 体验通过停止聊天响应和改进的代码完成等功能得到改进。
mainPoints.explanation: 诸如停止不需要的 AI 响应和“思考令牌”之类的新功能使开发人员能够更好地控制和洞察，而性能增强则简化了编码过程。

mainPoints.point: Gemini 命令行工具通过 GitHub Actions 和更深入的 VS Code 集成进行扩展，从而增强了自动化工作流程。
mainPoints.explanation: Gemini 命令行工具 GitHub Actions 的引入自动化了诸如问题分类和 PR 审查之类的例行编码任务，而增强的 VS Code 集成提供了上下文感知的建议和原生差异比较，从而提供更有针对性的帮助。

keyQuotes: IDE 中的代理模式可帮助您通过智能协作的方式处理复杂的多步骤任务。
通过将 AI 的强大功能与您的专业知识相结合，代理模式可帮助您实现更高效的工作流程并生成更好的代码，同时节省大量时间和精力。
由于具有停止进行中的聊天响应的新功能，您现在可以更好地控制与聊天的交互，因此您可以立即停止不需要的冗长或错误的聊天响应。
Gemini 命令行工具 GitHub Actions 现已推出，是您存储库的免费、功能强大的 AI 编码助手。
Gemini 命令行工具也已更新，并更深入地集成到 VS Code 中。它可以感知您打开的文件，并且可以访问选定的文本。
